root_text.an3.141:1.1: “Tayo ca, bhikkhave, assaparasse desessāmi tayo ca purisaparasse.
root_text.an3.141:1.2: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an3.141:1.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an3.141:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco assaparasso javasampanno hoti; na vaṇṇasampanno na ārohapariṇāhasampanno.
root_text.an3.141:2.3: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco assaparasso javasampanno hoti vaṇṇasampanno ca; na ārohapariṇāhasampanno.
root_text.an3.141:2.4: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco assaparasso javasampanno ca hoti vaṇṇasampanno ca ārohapariṇāhasampanno ca.
root_text.an3.141:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco purisaparasso javasampanno hoti; na vaṇṇasampanno na ārohapariṇāhasampanno.
root_text.an3.141:3.3: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco purisaparasso javasampanno ca hoti vaṇṇasampanno ca; na ārohapariṇāhasampanno.
root_text.an3.141:3.4: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco purisaparasso javasampanno ca hoti vaṇṇasampanno ca ārohapariṇāhasampanno ca.
root_text.an3.141:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, purisaparasso javasampanno hoti; na vaṇṇasampanno, na ārohapariṇāhasampanno?
root_text.an3.141:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an3.141:4.4: Abhidhamme kho pana abhivinaye pañhaṁ puṭṭho saṁsādeti, no vissajjeti.
root_text.an3.141:4.7: Idamassa na ārohapariṇāhasmiṁ vadāmi. Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, purisaparasso javasampanno hoti;
root_text.an3.141:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, purisaparasso javasampanno ca hoti vaṇṇasampanno ca, na ārohapariṇāhasampanno?
root_text.an3.141:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an3.141:5.4: Abhidhamme kho pana abhivinaye pañhaṁ puṭṭho vissajjeti, no saṁsādeti.
root_text.an3.141:5.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, purisaparasso javasampanno ca hoti; vaṇṇasampanno ca, na ārohapariṇāhasampanno.
root_text.an3.141:6.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, purisaparasso javasampanno ca hoti vaṇṇasampanno ca ārohapariṇāhasampanno ca?
root_text.an3.141:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.an3.141:6.4: Abhidhamme kho pana abhivinaye pañhaṁ puṭṭho vissajjeti, no saṁsādeti.
root_text.an3.141:6.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, purisaparasso javasampanno ca hoti vaṇṇasampanno ca ārohapariṇāhasampanno ca.

translation_text.an3.141:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the three excellent horses and the three excellent people.
translation_text.an3.141:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an3.141:2.2: One excellent horse is fast, but not beautiful or well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:2.3: Another excellent horse is fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:2.4: While another excellent horse is fast, beautiful, and well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:2.5: These are the three excellent horses.
translation_text.an3.141:3.2: One excellent person is fast, but not beautiful or well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:3.3: Another excellent person is fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:3.4: While another excellent person is fast, beautiful, and well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:4.1: And how is an excellent person fast, but not beautiful or well proportioned?
translation_text.an3.141:4.2: It’s when a mendicant, with the ending of the five lower fetters, is reborn spontaneously. They’re extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an3.141:4.3: This is how they’re fast, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:4.4: But when asked a question about the teaching or training, they falter without answering.
translation_text.an3.141:4.5: This is how they’re not beautiful, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:4.6: And they don’t receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an3.141:4.7: This is how they’re not well proportioned, I say. This is how an excellent person is fast,
translation_text.an3.141:4.8: but not beautiful or well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:5.1: And how is an excellent person fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned?
translation_text.an3.141:5.2: It’s when a mendicant, with the ending of the five lower fetters, is reborn spontaneously. They’re extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an3.141:5.3: This is how they’re fast, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:5.4: When asked a question about the teaching or training, they answer without faltering.
translation_text.an3.141:5.5: This is how they’re beautiful, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:5.6: But they don’t receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an3.141:5.7: This is how they’re not well proportioned, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:5.8: This is how an excellent person is fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:6.1: And how is an excellent person fast, beautiful, and well proportioned?
translation_text.an3.141:6.2: It’s when a mendicant, with the ending of the five lower fetters, is reborn spontaneously. They’re extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.an3.141:6.3: This is how they’re fast, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:6.4: When asked a question about the teaching or training, they answer without faltering.
translation_text.an3.141:6.5: This is how they’re beautiful, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:6.6: They receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an3.141:6.7: This is how they’re well proportioned, I say.
translation_text.an3.141:6.8: This is how an excellent person is fast, beautiful, and well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.141:6.9: These are the three excellent people.”

variant_text.an3.141:1.1: assaparasse → assasadasse (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | purisaparasse → purisasadasse (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
